vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues due to high avionics heat.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Loads from tilting rotor nacelles are transferred into the fuselage spine
Structural loads from wing spars are transferred into the fuselage structure
Heat from avionics bay is dissipated through the fuselage exterior
Motion from ruddervator is transmitted through the fuselage spine
Electrical signals from avionics bay are routed to flight control system
Human service access for avionics bay maintenance

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener torque leading to joint failure.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for nacelle tilt mechanism.
risk.mit: Ensure clearance is maintained during tilt mechanism operation.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly.
risk.mit: Use alignment tools and marks for precise assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of service access being obstructed.
risk.mit: Design for easy access to critical components.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust from 6309 bearing to wing spar via bearing seat and mounting flange
Heat from motor and ESC dissipates through nacelle shell to ambient air
Servo-driven worm gear rotates tilt axis allowing 0-90deg rotation
Servo and motor access through removable service covers
Maintenance access via removable service covers

internal_feature_targets.description: Arc for nacelle clearance during tilt
Removable cover for motor and ESC access

integration_opportunities.description: Support for 6309 bearing, critical for load transfer
Mechanical linkage for servo-driven tilt mechanism
Arc for nacelle clearance during tilt motion
Mounting point for tilt servo, critical for tilt actuation
Pocket for motor installation, critical for propulsion
Pocket for ESC installation, critical for motor control
Removable cover for motor and ESC access, critical for maintenance
Fins for heat dissipation, critical for thermal management

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum feature is a flat mounting flange
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: Tolerances must be maintained within ±0.2mm for critical interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures must be designed to minimize material waste and ensure part integrity
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Inspection must verify all critical dimensions and interfaces meet tolerance requirements

verification_focus.description: Ensure all mechanical interfaces and components meet load and cycle life requirements
Verify heat dissipation through thermal fins is sufficient for motor and ESC operation
Ensure all service access points are easily accessible and maintainable

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present in the part_tree and geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Each part_tree child must have at least 4 dedicated geometry_ops and reference it by id or target name.

verify.value: The mass estimate is within the expected range for a tiltrotor VTOL hybrid.
verify.rule: The cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal connections with neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate, and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature so the neighbor can declare the same joint family back.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence is present for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

verify.value: Fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces are defined for downstream CFD/FEA.
verify.rule: verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high ambient temperatures (-20 to +50C).
risk.mit: Implement thermal management systems such as heat sinks or cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of material fatigue due to cyclic loading from rotor thrust and servo actuation.
risk.mit: Conduct fatigue analysis and reinforce critical load paths.

risk.desc: Risk of clearance issues during assembly due to tolerance stack-up.
risk.mit: Perform detailed tolerance analysis and adjust clearances as necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient service access for maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Ensure all critical areas have adequate access panels and clearances.

risk.desc: Risk of aerodynamic instability due to improper ruddervator deflection.
risk.mit: Conduct wind tunnel tests and adjust ruddervator geometry if necessary.
